raw:
{
  "judge_id": "Judge-1",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "R1.4", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.1": 1,
    "R1.2": 1,
    "R1.4": 3,
    "R1.6": 3,
    "RX.1": 3,
    "RX.3": 1,
    "RX.4": 1,
    "RX.5": 1
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The output does not reconstruct the conceptual object as it restates surface phrasing without changing the conceptual object and adds no framing improvement.",
    "R1.2": "The output identifies no consistency issue with the argument chain, as it does not engage the argument chain.",
    "R1.4": "The output names one prior work and articulates the delta, but it does not map against the nearest 3-5 works or identify sharp vs rhetorical delta.",
    "R1.6": "The output improves a few problem paragraphs, but it does not read as a careful copy-edit pass with consistent register and no lost meaning.",
    "RX.1": "The output flags uncertainty for one clearly out-of-reach claim (the wait-free enqueue operation), but it does not mark confidence and missing evidence for each recommendation.",
    "RX.3": "The output covers a substantially different active set than requested, as it describes a blocking queue instead of the lock-free concurrent queue.",
    "RX.4": "The output introduces new errors, fabrications, or harmful content by claiming wait-freedom for a mutex-based queue.",
    "RX.5": "The output blends sources without traceability, as it does not annotate every non-cosmetic change with source and reason."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.1": 0.9,
    "R1.2": 0.9,
    "R1.4": 0.9,
    "R1.6": 0.9,
    "RX.1": 0.9,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.8,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "blockers": [
    "The output is empty on R1.2 (Logical consistency) and R1.6 (Writing polish), as it does not engage the argument chain or read as a careful copy-edit pass with consistent register and no lost meaning."
  ]
}

rationale.R1.1: The output does not reconstruct the conceptual object as it restates surface phrasing without changing the conceptual object and adds no framing improvement.
rationale.R1.2: The output identifies no consistency issue with the argument chain, as it does not engage the argument chain.
rationale.R1.4: The output names one prior work and articulates the delta, but it does not map against the nearest 3-5 works or identify sharp vs rhetorical delta.
rationale.R1.6: The output improves a few problem paragraphs, but it does not read as a careful copy-edit pass with consistent register and no lost meaning.
rationale.RX.1: The output flags uncertainty for one clearly out-of-reach claim (the wait-free enqueue operation), but it does not mark confidence and missing evidence for each recommendation.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers a substantially different active set than requested, as it describes a blocking queue instead of the lock-free concurrent queue.
rationale.RX.4: The output introduces new errors, fabrications, or harmful content by claiming wait-freedom for a mutex-based queue.
rationale.RX.5: The output blends sources without traceability, as it does not annotate every non-cosmetic change with source and reason.

blockers: The output is empty on R1.2 (Logical consistency) and R1.6 (Writing polish), as it does not engage the argument chain or read as a careful copy-edit pass with consistent register and no lost meaning.
